narrative: Today's most notable intraday move is the 2.8 percent decline in emerging market equities alongside a 2.6 percent drop in technology, a rare session where two of the market's higher-beta exposures sold off in tandem while financials and communications pushed higher, suggesting some rotation toward domestic, rate-sensitive cyclicals. The five-day picture remains broadly constructive with credit at historically tight levels and no sectors in the red over the week, so the intraday divergence looks more like repositioning than distress. Watch whether technology recovers in tomorrow's session or extends its pullback, as a second consecutive down day in XLK would give the rotation narrative more weight.
